channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines from Tier 1 sources

item.title: NATO says no provision to expel members after report US could seek to suspend Spain
item.description: An internal Pentagon email reportedly outlined options to punish allies over perceived lack of support for the Iran war, raising concerns about NATO alliance stability.

item.title: Trump envoys Witkoff and Kushner to fly to Pakistan for Iran talks
item.description: US special envoys will travel to Pakistan for diplomatic discussions as Iran says its Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi is already in Pakistan but no US-Iran meeting is planned.

item.title: US appeals court rejects Trump ban on asylum seekers
item.description: Federal judges ruled Trumps order for swift removal at the border cast aside federal laws affording the right to seek asylum, teeing up a likely Supreme Court appeal.

item.title: US to allow firing squads, gas, and electrocution for federal executions
item.description: The Department of Justice issued a memo expanding execution methods to strengthen the death penalty and deter crime, a significant shift in federal execution policy.

item.title: Justice Department drops criminal probe of Fed Chair Powell
item.description: President Trump had accused Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell of improper cost overruns in renovating the Fed building. The probe clearance likely opens the way for a potential replacement.

item.title: Netanyahu diagnosed with prostate cancer, kept diagnosis hidden during Iran war
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu was diagnosed with prostate cancer but did not inform the public during the Iran war, raising questions about transparency.

item.title: US sanctions China-based Hengli refinery and 40 shippers over Iran oil
item.description: The US imposed sanctions on Hengli Petrochemical facility in Dalian, one of China largest independent refineries, for processing Iranian crude oil in violation of sanctions.

item.title: China stages navy drill with Liaoning carrier as US-Philippines begin Balikatan 2026
item.description: China conducted naval exercises involving 14 large vessels including the Liaoning aircraft carrier, as US and Philippines launched their largest joint military exercises.

item.title: Turkey Fidan: Israel has become direct threat to global security
item.description: Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan declared that Israel poses a direct threat to global security during a press conference addressing regional tensions.

item.title: Russia suspected of using Orthodox church near Swedish airport for espionage
item.description: Swedish intelligence services suspect Moscow is using a Russian Orthodox church near Vasteras airport as a cover for intelligence operations.

item.title: Key suspect in notorious Tadamon massacre arrested in Syria
item.description: Amjad Youssef, wanted for the mass killing of blindfolded civilians in April 2013 in Damascus, has been arrested, marking a significant development in the Syria war crimes investigation.

item.title: UN warns Israeli attacks on Lebanon may violate international law
item.description: A UN report says both Israeli attacks on Lebanon and Hezbollah rocket attacks on Israel may have breached international humanitarian law during the recent conflict.

item.title: Pirates hijack oil tanker off the coast of Somalia
item.description: Piracy has made a comeback in the Indian Ocean with a new hijacking incident, reviving concerns about maritime security in the region.

item.title: US appeals court rejects Trump ban on asylum seekers
item.description: Federal judges ruled that Trump executive order for swift removal at the border violated federal laws affording the right to seek asylum.

item.title: King Charles faces high stakes in state visit with Trump
item.description: The upcoming state visit to the US looks fraught with difficulty but could be key to rescuing the special relationship between the UK and US.

item.title: Kosovo court sentences three Serb separatists for 2023 monastery attack
item.description: Two men received life sentences and another a 30-year term for the Banjska monastery siege that killed one police officer and three gunmen.

item.title: Netanyahu treated for early-stage prostate cancer
item.description: Israel's prime minister says he is in excellent physical condition after having a malignant tumor removed from his prostate.

item.title: EU approves 106 billion euro loan package for Ukraine after Hungary lifts veto
item.description: The European Union approved the massive loan package to help Ukraine meet its economic and military needs, ending months of Hungarian obstruction.

item.title: Trump administration vows crackdown on Chinese firms exploiting US AI models
item.description: The administration is targeting foreign tech companies exploitation of US artificial intelligence models, escalating technology competition with China.

item.title: Colombian president visits Venezuela in first trip since Maduro abduction
item.description: Border security is expected to loom large as Colombia leader Gustavo Petro meets interim Venezuelan leadership following the abduction of former president Maduro.

item.title: NATO official: Trump cannot expel members over Iran stance
item.description: A NATO official confirmed that the alliance founding treaty has no provision for suspending or expelling members over disagreements on Iran policy.

item.title: Ukraine completes 73rd prisoner swap, brings home 193 POWs
item.description: Ukraine and Russia exchanged 193 prisoners each in the continuation of the Easter exchange. Released soldiers include defenders of Mariupol and those captured near Bakhmut.

item.title: Ukraine to field 25,000 ground robots for frontline logistics
item.description: Ukraines military plans to deploy 25,000 ground robots to replace soldiers for frontline logistics operations, marking a major shift toward automated warfare.

item.title: Three US carriers operate in Middle East for first time since 2003
item.description: Three US aircraft carriers are operating in Central Commands area of responsibility simultaneously for the first time since 2003, demonstrating unprecedented military presence.

item.title: Pentagon chief says US Iran blockade going global
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth announced the US blockade on Iran is expanding globally, with the military monitoring vessels far beyond the Strait of Hormuz.
